title: linaria supina subsp. haenseleri
intro: Linaria supina subsp. haenseleri, also known as the woodland toadflax, is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae). Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been attributed to the presence of alkaloids such as gentianine and gentianidine. The health benefits of Linaria supina subsp. haenseleri include its potential to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to soothe digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to leverage its medicinal properties.
